title: Detach key interferes with shell history navigation in interactive container
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

With the default detach key combo `ctrl-p,ctrl-q`, shell history navigation (tested in bash and zsh) using `ctrl-p` to access the previous command will not display this command. However, adding additional characters to the command will cause it to be displayed, and using `ctrl-p` a second time will display the 2nd to last command.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -it <image> bash`

2. Enter a command: `echo`

3. Enter a second command: `test`

4. Press `ctrl-p`. Nothing will be displayed.

5. Type a character, e.g. `c`: `testc` will be displayed.
**or** Press `ctrl-p` again: `echo` will be displayed.

**Describe the results you received:**

Command history is not displayed properly.

**Describe the results you expected:**

Previous command should be displayed in shell.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.9
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Sorry for the delay here, the team's been very busy with Podman v2.0 over the last month.

This is an artifact of our detach key logic, which actually pauses sending any keystrokes until it confirms it is not the detach keys (IE, until a key has been pressed following the C-p, the code assumes it could be a C-p C-q, so sends nothing). I can't think of a way of altering that behaviour without making us send a C-p to the container when we're actually detaching, which could break things.

My initial suggesting would be to make `C-p C-p` send a single control-p into the container; making it two keys makes it unambiguous that it's not `C-p C-q` but still allows sending single a single press of the first key in the detach sequence. This has precedent from `tmux` (and I believe screen, but it's been years since I used it) where `C-b C-b` sends a single press of `C-b`.

Potential complexity: we allow reassigning detach keys, so someone could theoretically set `C-p C-p` as the actual detach keys. In that case, it may be reasonable to just disable the "send single keypress" behaviour (and tell people not to use a repeated key if they do want to be able to use it in the container).

Thanks for the thorough explanation.

My workaround is to use a detach key combo with an unconflicting first keystroke:
`detach_keys = "ctrl-q,ctrl-q"`

Sadly Docker picked a bad default.  Since the ^p key is used by default in bash for previous message.
You can use containers.conf to change the default for all containers.
```
# cat >> /etc/containers/containers.conf < _eof
[engine]
detach_keys="ctrl-q,ctrl-q"
_eof
```
